slides.title: The simplest heart habit might *have four legs*.
slides.sub: A dog pulls you outside every single day. That daily walk and steady company add up.

slides.title: A dog moves you daily
slides.body: A dog needs walking rain or shine. That gentle, non-negotiable routine *keeps you moving* on the days you might not.

slides.title: Lower heart risk
slides.body: Across large studies, dog ownership *is linked to* roughly a quarter lower risk of dying from cardiovascular disease.

slides.title: More steps, less sitting
slides.body: Owners log more daily steps and break up sitting more often. Consistent low-intensity movement *can support* a healthier heart.

slides.title: Companionship counts
slides.body: Steady company and routine may ease stress and loneliness, both of which *are linked to* better long-term heart health.

slides.title: It is the consistency
slides.body: You do not need a dog to get the benefit. A daily walk and real connection *do the heavy lifting* either way.

slides.title: Turn daily walks into *visible progress*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your steps, movement, sleep and food, then rolls it into one daily Health Score so each walk actually counts.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
The best heart habit might just be a leash by the door. 🐾🌿

A dog gets you walking every day, and that steady movement plus companionship is linked to lower heart risk. You do not need a dog to copy the recipe: daily walks and real connection. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns those daily walks into one simple score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Owning a Dog Is Quietly Good for Your Heart
